title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Corradino examines a swing door in Miss Blacklock's house that leads to a small living room. Miss Bonner explains that the door has been locked for a long time because the two living rooms were merged. After finding a key in a hall drawer, Corradino opens the door and finds that it slides open silently. He notes that the door has been opened recently and the hinges have been oiled, leading him to conclude that an intruder was present in the house on the night of the shooting. Corradino suggests that someone could have used this door to sneak up behind Rudolf Scherz and shoot him during the confusion caused by the lights going out.

When questioned about potential motives, Miss Blacklock identifies her nephew and niece, Patrick and Julia, as her heirs. She explains that she is not currently wealthy, living on taxes and low-interest investments, but she expects to become very wealthy soon. She reveals she was the secretary for the late millionaire Randall Goddard. Because Randall had no children, he left his assets to his wife, Bella, who in turn left them to Miss Blacklock. Miss Blacklock notes that Bella is currently dying in Scotland, meaning Miss Blacklock will inherit the fortune in a few weeks. However, if Miss Blacklock were to die before Bella, the money would go to Randall's sister, Sonia, or Sonia's husband, a man Miss Blacklock describes as a scoundrel named Dimitri Stanfordthis. Corradino questions whether Randall had removed Sonia from his will following her marriage.
